rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly frames its contribution as extending defensive processing research and tests defense-reduction interventions (self-affirmation, fear voicing), while the cited 2010 JMR paper’s core contribution is a defensive-processing mechanism explaining persuasion backfire in health messaging. Given this strong construct/mechanism overlap (not just topical similarity), the citation is plausibly theoretical/mechanistic rather than an incidental mention, despite only DOI-level matched reference evidence.
